key_insight: MXtalGFlow turns molecular crystal generation into sampling a thermodynamically meaningful Boltzmann distribution, rather than producing isolated candidate structures.
engineering_relevance: This provides a principled way to explore polymorphism and prioritize experimentally relevant crystal packings for materials discovery and structure prediction.
method: Defines a canonical crystal parameterization for molecular crystals and space groups.
Trains energy-based Generative Flow Networks (GFlowNets) to match a target Boltzmann distribution over crystal structures.
Analyzes sampled distributions to identify local structural basins and competitive alternative packing modes.
evidence_signal: The framework is evaluated on two molecules under two energy models (Lennard-Jones and the Universal Model for Atoms), recovering known polymorph basins and additional plausible packing modes.
watch_item: Results depend on the chosen energy function and space-group/parameterization choices, so distribution quality may be sensitive to modeling assumptions.
paper_title: A Distributional Framework for Generative Modeling of Molecular Crystals
